feedback.text: Hello, I am a Citizen in Luxembourg and I wanted to make you all attentive to a certain issue. Lately some Companies have started to kill off video games that people have already bought. This issue became a real problem when Ubisoft a French/Canadian developer shut down a game called "The Crew" it was a game people paid full price whihc was 60 euros for most people who bought it and Ubisoft just out of nowhere with no warning whatsoever completely shut the game down without people ever getting their money back. This issue is becoming a bigger problem in general as even certain games that we are buying nowadays are just a licence to be able to play said game and those gaming companies are not mentioning it on purpose so people pay full price for it not knowing that they could lose their game at any moment if any of those companies decides to just close it down. If something has been bought it my my belief that the person who paid for it has a right to own the product that was bought. It's like if you bought a new car and then after 2 years because the car manufacturer just installed a shut down feature in the car for you to not be able to use it anymore because they just dont want to support that model anymore. With every passing year more and more content is becoming digitalized and the common user has no defensive stance against it forcing some people to even pirate it just in case a company may take if away from the person. This does not work for every game sadly as the previously mentioned "the crew" was online only and if people nowadays try to play the game it just doesnt work because they just completely turned it off with people who bought the game having no other way to use the content that they paid for. So i would kindly ask of you to please consider helping video games too, it is an art form that mixes creation and interactivity like no other and it is quite upsetting that some of those games will never be able to exist anymore because for some companies they arent making enough money of that product anymore to the point that even the people who bought it arent allowed to enjoy it anymore. So please help us save our video games too so that hopefully the stuff people bought years ago can continue to live on. Thank you for reading this
